Daily Focus (4 hrs):
- Team meeting (30 min): Discuss goals, assign roles, establish communication
- Research open-source Python Arcade games (1.5 hrs): Star Wars, Space Shooter, Flappy Bird, etc.
- Choose game & download (30 min): Test on all team members' machines
- Create GitHub repo & add README template (1 hr)
- Initial setup complete: Game runs on all machines

- All team members: Clone repo & run game locally (30 min)
- Team Lead: Create project directory structure, add requirements.txt (1 hr)
- Map out codebase: Identify where visuals, gameplay, sprites are defined (1.5 hrs)
- Plan modifications: Decide exactly what changes each dev will make (1 hr)
- Document initial findings in shared doc

- Developer 1: Identify visual asset files & color variables (1.5 hrs)
- Developer 2: Locate gameplay logic & speed/scoring variables (1.5 hrs)
- Team Lead: Document code sections, create dev guide (1 hr)
- All: Attend class & discuss project if applicable (flexible)

- Team Lead: Set up Git branches for each developer (30 min)
- All team: Git workflow training - commits, branches, merging (1.5 hrs)
- Developer 1: Create feature branch for visual changes (30 min)
- Developer 2: Create feature branch for gameplay changes (30 min)
- Team Lead: Document code review checklist in README (1 hr)

- Review Week 1 progress (30 min)
- Each dev: Write pseudocode for their modifications (1.5 hrs)
- Team Lead: Update README with team structure, initial findings (1 hr)
- Test game one more time on all machines (1 hr)
- Prepare Week 2 sprint checklist

- Review visual code section (30 min)
- Make first visual change (e.g., background color) (1.5 hrs)
- Test change in-game (1 hr)
- Document change with inline comments (1 hr)
- Commit to feature branch

- Review gameplay code section (30 min)
- Make first gameplay change (e.g., enemy speed or scoring) (1.5 hrs)
- Test change during gameplay (1 hr)
- Document change with inline comments (1 hr)
- Commit to feature branch

- Developer 1: Refine visual change, test on different screen sizes (1.5 hrs)
- Developer 2: Refine gameplay change, test edge cases (1.5 hrs)
- Team Lead: Code review both changes, check comments (1 hr)
- All: Sync up, discuss progress & blockers (30 min)

- Identify code section needing refactoring (30 min)
- Add documentation/comments to function (1.5 hrs)
- Improve code clarity (organize variables, better naming) (1.5 hrs)
- Create feature branch & commit changes (30 min)
- Attend class & milestone check-in if applicable

- Test game with ALL changes integrated (1 hr)
- Fix any merge conflicts or compatibility issues (1 hr)
- Create a test checklist document (30 min)
- Prepare standup presentation for instructor (1.5 hrs)
- Sprint retrospective: What went well, what needs work

- Standup meeting with instructor Shay (30 min - 1 hr)
- Address feedback from standup (1 hr)
- Comprehensive game testing: Visual + Gameplay + Refactor (1 hr)
- Test on different systems if possible (30 min)
- Document any bugs found

- Developer 1: Write detailed description of visual change (1 hr)
- Developer 2: Write detailed description of gameplay change (1 hr)
- Team Lead: Create before-and-after code snippets (1 hr)
- Add screenshots or descriptions of changes (1 hr)
- Review README for clarity

- Write comprehensive testing section (30 min)
- Create troubleshooting guide (1 hr)
- Verify all file paths & instructions work (1 hr)
- Final README review & edits (1.5 hrs)
- Ensure all inline code comments are complete

- Team Lead: Prepare intro slides (game overview, team roles) (1.5 hrs)
- Developer 1: Prepare visual change demo script (1 hr)
- Developer 2: Prepare gameplay change demo script (1 hr)
- All: Practice individual 1-2 min sections (30 min)
- Attend class & continue with coursework

- Full game playthrough on presentation computer (1 hr)
- Test all modifications work as expected (1 hr)
- Confirm GitHub repo is up-to-date (30 min)
- Check that game runs without errors (1.5 hrs)
- Prepare emergency backup (USB, cloud backup)

- Full team presentation run-through (2 hrs)
- Time each section - stay within 5-minute total (30 min)
- Polish delivery & speaking clarity (1 hr)
- Get feedback from peers or mentor (30 min)
- Adjust based on feedback

Task Title: Final Code Review & Quality Check (4 hrs)
Daily Focus (4 hrs):
- Code quality review: PEP 8 compliance, clear naming (1 hr)
- Ensure all inline comments are helpful & clear (1 hr)
- Final testing run: Game stability & all changes visible (1 hr)
- Create final commit with message 'Project complete' (30 min)
- Tag release version (v1.0)

- Full dress rehearsal with timing (2 hrs)
- Test game launch on presentation computer (1 hr)
- Prepare contingency plan if tech fails (30 min)
- Finalize any last-minute edits (30 min)
- Mental preparation for presentation

- Final README review & GitHub check (1 hr)
- Verify project meets all technical requirements (1 hr)
- Create submission checklist document (30 min)
- Submit final project to instructor (30 min)
- Prepare reflection statements for grading

- Arrive early, test game one final time (1 hr)
- Demo the game to any interested classmates pre-presentation (1 hr)
- Give 5-minute presentation to class (5 min) ← This is the actual presentation
- Answer Q&A from instructor/classmates (15 min)
- Celebrate! Document lessons learned (1.5 hrs remaining)
